passages.title: テレワークという働き方
body: るため、コミュニケーションが
があいまいになり、かえって
じている。テレワークか
わせることこそが、これからの
められているのではないだろうか。
passages.en:
In recent years, a style of working called telework has spread rapidly. Because there is no need to commute to the office and work can be done at home, it is said to have many advantages, such as reducing commuting time and making it easier to balance work with child-rearing.
On the other hand, there are also quite a few problems. Since there are fewer opportunities to meet colleagues face to face, communication tends to become insufficient. In particular, employees who have just joined a company are said to feel anxious easily because they cannot casually ask senior colleagues how to proceed with their work. Also, the line between work and private life becomes blurred, and some people reportedly end up working longer hours instead.
The author also works from home half the week. While the time I can concentrate has increased, I feel that the ideas born from casual chatting have decreased. Rather than an either-or choice between telework and going to the office, isn't skillfully combining both according to the nature of the work exactly what is required of the way we work from now on?

questions.q: テレワークの利点として本文で挙げられているものはどれか。
choices: 給料が上がること
通勤時間が減ること
同僚と直接話す機会が増えること
早く昇進できること

questions.q: テレワークの課題として本文の内容に合うものはどれか。
choices: 仕事の量が減ってしまうこと
会社のオフィスが狭くなること
仕事と生活の区別があいまいになること
通勤時間が長くなること

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: テレワークをやめて全員が出社すべきだ
すべての仕事をテレワークにするべきだ
雑談は仕事に必要ないものだ
仕事の内容に応じて出社とテレワークを組み合わせるべきだ

passages.title: プラスチックごみと海
body: われている。しかし、その
てられたプラスチックごみの
かれ、マイクロプラスチックと
れないかもしれない。しかし、
passages.en:
It would be fair to say that our lives could not function without plastic products. Light, durable and cheap, plastic is used in every situation, such as packaging and containers. Behind this convenience, however, a serious environmental problem is occurring.
Part of the discarded plastic waste flows into the sea through rivers. Waste that reaches the sea is broken into tiny pieces by waves and ultraviolet light, becoming small particles called microplastics. Fish swallow them, and since humans eat those fish, effects on the human body are also feared.
To solve this problem, changes in each individual's behavior are indispensable, not to mention efforts by governments and companies. Small actions such as bringing your own shopping bag or avoiding disposable containers may not save the ocean by themselves. However, the author believes that society changes only when the small actions of many people come together.

questions.q: マイクロプラスチックはどのようにしてできるか。
choices: 工場で意図的に作られる
海に流れ込んだごみが波や紫外線によって細かく砕かれてできる
魚の体の中で作られる
買い物袋が溶けてできる

questions.q: プラスチックが広く使われている理由として本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 環境に優しいから
高級な印象を与えるから
軽くて丈夫で安いから
再利用が簡単だから

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 多くの人の小さな行動が集まることで社会は変わる
個人の行動には全く意味がない
国と企業だけが取り組めば問題は解決する
プラスチックの使用をただちに全面的に禁止すべきだ

passages.title: 高齢化社会をどうとらえるか
body: すきっかけとしてとらえるべきだと
passages.en:
In Japan, the proportion of elderly people in the total population is rising year by year. At the same time, the number of children being born keeps declining, and the aging society with a falling birthrate has become one of the biggest challenges facing Japanese society.
As aging advances, the working generation shrinks and the burden of social security, such as pensions and medical care, grows heavier. In rural areas, because young people move to the cities, shops and hospitals close, and the number of regions where daily life becomes inconvenient is increasing.
On the other hand, it would not be right to regard the elderly merely as people to be supported. Elderly people, with their years of experience and knowledge, are also valuable human resources for local communities. In fact, quite a few people play active roles in community activities and volunteer work after retirement.
The author believes that aging should be seen not simply as a problem but as an opportunity to rethink the structure of society. A society in which everyone, regardless of age, can participate by making use of their abilities — is that not exactly what Japan should aim for from now on?

questions.q: 高齢化が進むと起こることとして、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 子供の数が増える
働く世代が増える
年金の負担が軽くなる
社会保障の負担が重くなる

questions.q: 地方で起きていることとして、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 若者が都市に出ていき、店や病院が閉まっている
高齢者が都市に移住している
病院の数が増え続けている
若者の人口が増えている

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 高齢者は支えられるだけの存在である
高齢化は社会の仕組みを見直すきっかけとしてとらえるべきだ
高齢化の問題は地方だけの問題である
定年後はボランティアを義務にすべきだ

passages.title: 書評：『時間と生き方』
passages.en:
The other day I read a book called 'Time and How to Live.' The author is a psychologist who has spent many years studying how modern people use their time.
At the heart of the book is the claim that 'being busy does not necessarily mean being fulfilled.' According to the author, we try to gain a sense of security by packing our schedules, but as a result we lose the room to savor each individual experience. What impressed me most was the suggestion to set aside just ten minutes at the end of the day to do nothing. It sounds simple, but when I actually tried it, it was surprisingly difficult, and it made me realize how much I am in a state of 'feeling anxious unless I am doing something.'
The explanations are somewhat over-theoretical, and in the second half the same points are repeated. The price is not cheap, either. Even so, for people who feel chased by time, I would recommend this book as a chance to stop for a moment and take a fresh look at their own lives.

questions.q: 『時間と生き方』の著者はどんな人物か。
choices: 小説家
経済学者
心理学者
医者

questions.q: 本書の中心にある主張はどれか。
choices: 予定は多ければ多いほどよい
忙しさは必ずしも充実を意味しない
時間は誰にとっても平等である
現代人はもっと働くべきだ

questions.q: この文章を書いた筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 本書には欠点がなく、完璧な本である
提案は簡単で、誰でもすぐに実行できる
本書は読む価値がないと思う
欠点はあるが、時間に追われている人に本書を勧めたい

passages.title: 名刺交換の作法
passages.en:
In Japanese business settings, exchanging business cards is considered not merely an exchange of contact information but an important form of greeting. Because it can influence a first impression, you will want to master the basic etiquette.
As a rule, the person of lower status, or the visiting side, offers their card first. When handing over a card, take it out of your card case, turn it so the other person can read it, and present it with both hands. At that moment, do not forget to clearly state your company name and your own name. When receiving a card, also take it with both hands, and it is good to add a word such as 'chodai itashimasu' (I humbly accept it).
Putting away a card you have just received immediately is considered rude. During the meeting, it is regarded as polite to place it on top of your card case on the desk and continue the conversation while confirming the other person's name. You should also avoid writing memos on the other person's card on the spot.
There is no need to be bound by formality alone, but understanding that behind these manners lies respect for the other person is probably the most important thing of all.

questions.q: 名刺は誰から先に差し出すのが原則か。
choices: 目下の者、あるいは訪問した側
目上の者、あるいは訪問を受けた側
年齢が上の人
どちらから出してもよい

questions.q: 受け取った名刺の扱い方として、丁寧だとされているものはどれか。
choices: すぐにかばんにしまう
その場でメモを書き込む
名刺入れの上にのせて机に置き、名前を確認しながら話す
相手に返す

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 名刺交換の形式さえ守れば十分である
作法の背景にある相手への敬意を理解することが最も重要だ
名刺交換はもうやめるべき習慣だ
名刺は片手で渡してもかまわない

passages.title: 和食と世界
body: めている。すしやラーメンはもちろん、
のカレーやラーメンも、
れないことではないだろうか。
passages.en:
In recent years, Japanese food culture has been attracting attention all over the world. Not only sushi and ramen but washoku itself has been praised as a healthy diet, and the number of Japanese restaurants overseas keeps increasing year by year. The fact that washoku was registered as an international cultural heritage also gave this trend a push.
On the other hand, the Japanese food served overseas is not necessarily the same as that in Japan. In many cases, the flavors and ingredients are freely changed to suit local people's tastes. Some people criticize such changes as 'not authentic,' but if you think about it, Japanese curry and ramen are themselves dishes that came from abroad and were remade to suit Japanese tastes.
Food culture is something that has always kept changing along with the movement of people. The author believes that rather than fearing changes in form, we should enjoy the new charms born through change. Isn't the important thing not to forget our respect for the culture behind each dish?

questions.q: 海外の日本食レストランについて、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 数が年々減っている
数が年々増え続けている
日本と全く同じ味を出している
健康に悪いと批判されている

questions.q: 海外の日本食の味や材料が変えられているのはなぜか。
choices: 日本の材料が輸入できないから
作り方が正しく伝わっていないから
現地の人の好みに合わせるため
法律で義務づけられているから

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 海外の日本食は本物ではないので認めるべきではない
和食は変化させずに守ることが最も重要だ
変化を楽しみつつ、料理の背景にある文化への敬意を忘れるべきではない
日本のカレーやラーメンは和食とは言えない

passages.title: 都会と田舎、どちらに住むか
body: めることはできないのである。
するのかをはっきりさせることだ。
ぶことにほかならないと
passages.en:
Recently, it is said that more and more people are leaving the cities and moving to rural areas. In cities, jobs and shops are concentrated and transportation is convenient. However, rents are high, commuter trains are crowded, and there are few opportunities to be in touch with nature. It may be a natural trend that people who are tired of their busy lives and seek breathing room turn their eyes to the countryside.
On the other hand, rural life has its own problems. The types of jobs are limited, and because trains and buses run infrequently, life is difficult without a car. In some areas hospitals and schools are far away, and quite a few people feel anxious about raising children or growing old there. There are also hardships not found in the city, such as shoveling snow in winter.
In short, it is impossible to decide in general terms which is better, the city or the countryside. What matters is to clarify what you yourself value — income, nature, connections with people, and so on. The author believes that choosing where to live is nothing other than choosing what kind of life you want to lead.

questions.q: 都会の生活について、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 家賃が安い
自然と触れ合う機会が多い
仕事や店が集中し、交通が便利である
電車がすいている

questions.q: 田舎の生活の課題として、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 家賃が高すぎること
通勤電車が混むこと
店が多すぎること
車がなければ生活しにくいこと

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 都会より田舎のほうが必ず優れている
住む場所を選ぶことは、どんな人生を送りたいかを選ぶことだ
収入よりも自然を重視すべきだ
地方への移住はやめるべきだ

passages.title: キャッシュレス決済と私たちの生活
body: をするキャッシュレス
がっている。カードやスマートフォンをかざすだけで
がないわけではない。まず、
いそのものができなくなる
もふだんはキャッシュレス
るのではなく、それぞれの
passages.en:
Cashless payment, in which you shop without using cash, is spreading rapidly in Japan as well. Since payment is completed just by holding up a card or smartphone, the time spent waiting in line at the register becomes shorter, and there is no need to carry a wallet. For stores, too, there is the advantage that the work of counting cash is reduced.
However, that is not to say there are no problems. First, it is hard to use for elderly people who are not used to operating machines. Also, if a power outage or communication failure occurs, there is a risk that payment itself becomes impossible. Furthermore, it is said that some people lose the sense of having spent money and end up spending too much without meaning to.
The author also usually uses cashless payment, but always carries a certain amount of cash in preparation for disasters. Rather than relying only on convenience, the author believes it is important to understand the strengths and weaknesses of each payment method and use them appropriately depending on the situation.

questions.q: キャッシュレス決済の利点として、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: レジに並ぶ時間が短くなること
現金が増えること
停電の時も使えること
高齢者に使いやすいこと

questions.q: キャッシュレス決済の課題として、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 店側の手間が増えること
財布が必要になること
支払いに時間がかかること
通信障害が起きると支払いができなくなる恐れがあること

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 現金は全く必要なくなった
キャッシュレス決済は使うべきではない
支払い方法の長所と短所を理解し、状況に応じて使い分けるべきだ
高齢者もすぐにキャッシュレスに変えるべきだ

passages.title: 宿題は必要か
body: えるべきではないだろうか。
passages.en:
In recent years, the debate over whether elementary school students need homework has become lively. People who oppose homework argue that making children who have already studied for long hours at school study even more at home is too heavy a burden. Their view is that time spent with family and time spent learning through play are exactly what is indispensable for children's growth.
On the other hand, people who support homework state that reviewing at home helps what was learned take hold. There is also the opinion that the habit of sitting down at a desk at a fixed time every day leads, in the future, to the ability to plan and learn on one's own.
The author thinks the problem lies not in whether homework exists but in its content. If homework merely makes children write the same kanji dozens of times, it will only make them hate studying. Conversely, homework that has them investigate the mysteries around them can teach the joy of learning. Before debating whether to assign homework, shouldn't we be thinking about what kind of homework can develop children's abilities?

questions.q: 宿題に反対する人々の主張として、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 家でさらに勉強させるのは子供の負担が大きすぎる
宿題は先生の負担になる
宿題は簡単すぎる
漢字の宿題だけを増やすべきだ

questions.q: 宿題に賛成する人々の意見として、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 宿題があると家族の時間が増える
宿題は遊びより楽しい
家庭で復習することで学んだ内容が定着しやすくなる
宿題がないと学校が困る

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 宿題はすべてなくすべきだ
漢字を何十回も書く宿題が最もよい
宿題の量を今より増やすべきだ
どんな宿題なら子供の力を伸ばせるのかを考えるべきだ

passages.title: 「限定」という言葉の力
body: やインターネットで、こうした
いわけではない。しかし、「
passages.en:
'Limited time only.' 'Only a few left.' Not a day goes by without seeing such phrases in stores or on the internet. In fact, these words are known to have a strong power to move consumers' hearts. It is said that people feel higher value in things they can only obtain now than in things they can buy anytime.
For example, there is a case in which a confectionery maker put the same product on sale as 'winter only,' and sales grew significantly. Even though the contents of the product had not changed, just by adding the word 'limited,' consumers felt it was something special.
Of course, not all such sales methods are bad. However, it is a problem if, flustered by the word 'limited,' we end up buying things we do not really need. Before buying, the author would like to recommend asking yourself, 'Would I still want this even if it were not limited?' That one small pause leads to shopping you will not regret.

questions.q: 「限定」という言葉について、本文に合うものはどれか。
choices: 消費者の心を動かす強い力がある
法律で使用が禁止されている
高齢者にしか効果がない
売り上げを減らすことが多い

questions.q: 菓子メーカーの例で、売り上げが伸びたのはなぜか。
choices: 商品の中身がよくなったから
値段を下げたから
「限定」という言葉によって消費者が特別なものだと感じたから
有名人が宣伝したから

questions.q: 筆者の考えに合うものはどれか。
choices: 限定商品は必ず買うべきだ
買う前に本当に欲しいのか自分に問いかけてみるべきだ
限定という言葉を使う店では買い物をしてはいけない
買い物で後悔するのは仕方がない
